@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "valkey-1936-new-negative-filtering-for-client-command-filters",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "valkey: [NEW] Negative filtering for client command filters",
+    "description": "[NEW] Negative filtering for client command filters. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current valkey setup",
+        "description": "Verify your valkey version and configuration:\n```bash\nvalkey-server --version\n```\nThis feature requires a working valkey installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review valkey configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant valkey configuration:\ndoes this PR support negative filtering?\n\n_Originally posted by  in https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/issues/1466#issuecomment-2647089232_"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [NEW] Negative filtering for client command filters",
+        "description": "introduce negative filters for `CLIENT LIST` and `CLIENT KILL` commands:\n\n1. `NOT-ID`: Excludes clients in the IDs set\n2. `NOT-TYPE`: Excludes clients of the specified type\n3. `NOT-ADDR`: Excludes clients of the specified address and port\n4. `NOT-LADDR`: Excludes clients connected to the specified\n```yaml\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill ADDR ~ 127.0.0.1:23456  // Kill all clients except 127.0.0.1:23456\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill TYPE ~ NORMAL  // Kill all clients except NORMAL type\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill USER ~ user1  // Kill all clients except user1\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill NAME ~ valkey  // Kill all clients except named valkey\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill SKIPME YES/NO // Do not need filtering\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill ID ~ client-id-1 client-id-2  // Kill all clients except id-1 and id-2\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill MAXAGE maxage // Do not need filtering, but we can add new option like:\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade valkey to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade valkey:\n```bash\n# Check current version\nvalkey-server --version\n# Follow the project's upgrade guide at https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"[NEW] Negative filtering for client command filters\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "introduce negative filters for `CLIENT LIST` and `CLIENT KILL` commands:\n\n1. `NOT-ID`: Excludes clients in the IDs set\n2. `NOT-TYPE`: Excludes clients of the specified type\n3. `NOT-ADDR`: Excludes clients of the specified address and port\n4. `NOT-LADDR`: Excludes clients connected to the specified local address and port\n5. `NOT-USER`: Excludes clients of the specified user\n6.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "127.0.0.1:6379> client kill ADDR ~ 127.0.0.1:23456  // Kill all clients except 127.0.0.1:23456\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill TYPE ~ NORMAL  // Kill all clients except NORMAL type\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill USER ~ user1  // Kill all clients except user1\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill NAME ~ valkey  // Kill all clients except named valkey\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill SKIPME YES/NO // Do not need filtering\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill ID ~ client-id-1 client-id-2  // Kill all clients except id-1 and id-2\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill MAXAGE maxage // Do not need filtering, but we can add new option like: client kill AGE >10|<=600\n127.0.0.1:6379> client kill FLAGS ~ flag // Kill all clients except flag",
+        "CLIENT LIST [ TYPE normal | master | replica | pubsub ] [ ID client-id ] [ [ ID client-id ] ... ] [ USER username ] [ NOT-USER username ] [ ADDR addr ] [ NOT-ADDR addr ] [ LADDR laddr ] [ SKIPME YES | NO ] [ MAXAGE maxage ] [ NAME name ] [ IDLE idle ] [ FLAGS flags ] [ LIB-NAME lib-name ] [ LIB-VER lib-ver ] [ DB db ] [ CAPA capa ] [ IP ip ]"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "valkey",
+      "community",
+      "cache",
+      "feature"
+    ],

+    },

+  },

+      "valkey-server",
+      "valkey-cli"
+    ],

+  },
